title: Investigate and Fix Slow Page Loading Causes
description:
Investigate causes of slow page loading times across the application. Two issues were identified and resolved:

### Issue 1: New Relic Script Auto-Injection
New Relic monitoring code was being auto-injected into every page from a Cloudways server-level configuration (not from the application source code). Removing it from source code had no effect until the Cloudways-level injection was also disabled.

### Issue 2: Gravity Form AJAX Request
The "All Tutorials" tab fetches content from the datatoolspro.com/support page, which includes a Gravity Form. The form's AJAX request took ~3 seconds to complete, adding significant latency. The form was stripped from the fetched content since it's not needed for the tutorial links display.

### Result
Page load time reduced by approximately 3 seconds after both fixes.
acceptance_criteria: New Relic auto-injection is disabled at the Cloudways level
Gravity Form AJAX request no longer delays page loading
Overall page load time is measurably reduced
middleware_changes: Stripped Gravity Form HTML when fetching tutorial content from the support page to avoid unnecessary AJAX requests.
implementation_notes: New Relic was being injected at the Cloudways server level, not from application code. Removing it from the codebase alone was insufficient. The Gravity Form on the support page caused a ~3 second AJAX delay when its HTML was fetched into the app.
comment_summary: Developer identified two causes: New Relic code being auto-injected from Cloudways (not source code), and a Gravity Form AJAX request adding ~3 seconds. Both were fixed and deployed to production.
